title: Synthetic Identity Seasoning with Micro-Payment Cultivation and Rapid Bust-Out
narrative: Synthetic identity establishes legitimacy through 6-9 months of predictable micro-payments ($8-35) on utility MCCs (4900, 4814) with perfect on-time behavior. Account demonstrates card1_txn_count_30d consistently at 4-6 transactions monthly. TransactionAmt variance remains under $12 for entire seasoning period. Sudden behavioral shift occurs: within 72-hour window, account executes 15-23 transactions totaling $4,800-$8,200 across high-risk MCCs (5732 electronics, 5944 jewelry). TransactionAmt jumps to $280-$650 range with card1_amt_zscore spiking from 0.2 to 4.7. Bust-out phase shows is_night flag on 68% of transactions, dist1 values exceeding 850 miles from established addr1 pattern, and switch from verified P_emaildomain (gmail.com) to disposable domains. No chargeback disputes filed during seasoning, then complete payment default post-bust-out.
indicators: 6-9 months of consistent micro-payments $8-35 on utility MCCs followed by 72-hour burst of 15-23 transactions
card1_amt_zscore spike from <0.5 during seasoning to >4.0 during bust-out phase
TransactionAmt variance under $12 for 180+ days then sudden $280-$650 individual charges
dist1 changes from <50 miles to 850+ miles between seasoning and bust-out transactions
is_night transaction percentage increases from 12% to 68% during high-value burst
MCC shift from utilities (4900, 4814) to electronics/jewelry (5732, 5944) within same 72-hour window
P_emaildomain switches from established provider to disposable domain concurrent with spending spike
summary: An account maintains 6-9 months of small, predictable payments ($8-35) on utility bills with perfect payment history, then suddenly executes 15-23 high-value transactions ($280-650) within 72 hours at electronics and jewelry merchants, often at night and from distant locations.
reasoning: Fraudsters build a fake identity's credit profile through carefully controlled "good" behavior to gain trust and credit limits, then maximize unauthorized purchases before abandoning the account—the drastic behavioral shift from patient micro-payments to rapid high-value spending reveals the orchestrated nature of the scheme.
indicator_explanations: Account builds trust with tiny bills then explodes into shopping spree
Spending suddenly rockets far beyond the carefully established normal pattern
Charges stay tiny and predictable for months then jump to luxury amounts
Card travels hundreds of miles away from its established geographic comfort zone
Activity shifts from daytime legitimate hours to overnight fraud-friendly timing
Purchases flip from utility bills to high-resale electronics and jewelry instantly
Email changes from credible provider to burner address right before attack
